meaning: n.杂耍,穿插表演,附属事件
examples: In the end, the meeting was a sideshow to a political storm that broke Thursday.
He ended up on a circus sideshow traveling through the country with the big steel pipe that went through his head.
But this is mostly a sideshow.
Prof Hangst said the antigravity measurement was just an "interesting sideshow" for the experiment.
He calls these destinations "midways, " as the sideshow areas of fairs were once known.
